title: lespedeza thunbergii subsp. formosa
intro: Lespedeza thunbergii subsp. formosa, also known as the Korean or Japanese bush clover, is a perennial legume native to East Asia, characterized by its clusters of small, fragrant flowers and slender stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which are believed to help alleviate conditions such as fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Lespedeza thunbergii subsp. formosa include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve respiratory function, and boost the immune system. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
